question-marks.title: Question Marks
question-marks.prompt: High market growth, low relative share. Which units deserve selective investment, and which should be abandoned before they consume too much cash?
examples: Fast-growing category where we are a small challenger
New product with market momentum but weak share
Promising geography where leadership is still uncertain

stars.title: Stars
stars.prompt: High market growth, high relative share. Which units require investment to defend leadership and become future cash generators?
examples: Category leader in a fast-growing market
Platform business with rapid adoption and strong position
Brand with share leadership and expanding demand

dogs.title: Dogs
dogs.prompt: Low market growth, low relative share. Which units should be divested, closed, repositioned, or kept only for strategic reasons?
examples: Legacy product in a flat market with weak share
Local brand with no path to leadership

cash-cows.title: Cash Cows
cash-cows.prompt: Low market growth, high relative share. Which units should be managed for cash, defended efficiently, and used to fund future bets?
examples: Mature category leader with high margins
Stable franchise that funds H2/H3 growth options

portfolio-actions.title: Portfolio actions
portfolio-actions.prompt: Translate quadrant positions into actions: invest, harvest, select, divest, reposition, or validate the market/share assumptions.
examples: Invest in two Question Marks, harvest one Cash Cow, divest one Dog
Recheck market growth using external data
Expand one Star into BMC and funding plan
